Deeply understand this. and build me a 20 page work book with left side and right side in each image of the pages, as well as cover, back cover, promotional materials etc. Where i'm teaching them 

As a note, i lost 70lbs in 4 months, have maintained my weight consistantly, growing muscle, and  using this method, and am continuing to shape a program to help others. 

My name is Justin Massion. this is me. and some friends and things you can use to make this fun. feel free to change my outfits, and so on.  Progressive Overload, Progressive Progress, etc. Be sure to include things that customers can fill in the page, and integrate the forms and stuff into the artwork and illustrations in an interesting way. You can make the pages cinematic quality, and the forms and such can feel like video game ui built into the page, but make it easy for people to print and fill in. on super high quality paper and color ink.

One of the things we can do is work with each willy wonka actor and oompa loompas when describing the supplements and low cal solutions,  We can use manrique the male manequin and manriqua the female wood manequin to show that all humans are pretty similar, we all run on calories, and other things, then there's slight differences as we start to add the genetics and nature/nurture,  etc. 

Use Garfield as an example for showing how understading caloric density is key to filling your stomach without putting on weight.  Use other characters to describe consistancy.  Use other famous characters to describe the math of quantity of reps and weight and progressive overload as a minimal way to grow strength and so on. And how to choose the least amount of exercises to get most gain with compound movements, etc. 

Feel free to invite characters from fiction, and non-fiction to support the poitns. At the end of the book, make a gallery of a whole bunch more lessons with famous characters and personalities coming soon in future books. 

Also, visualize your replies in this chat thread as text on a branded page, so i can read what you reply inside the canvas on beautiful letterheads or art
